title: Honey-Dijon Chicken Bean Bake with Sumac Onions & Herb Salad
summary: Another speedy midweek dinner using up your leftover Honey-Dijon Chicken & Pepper Traybake leftovers. The sumac onions and fresh herbs really take it to the next level for a weeknight.

method.description: Before you begin, make the Honey-Dijon Chicken & Pepper Traybake batch recipe.
Heat the oven to 200°C. Drain the butter beans, reserving a little of the bean liquid.

Add the beans and traybake vegetables to a baking tray with the chicken stock. Stir together, adding a couple of splashes of bean liquid if you want it saucier. Place the chicken on top. Slide into the oven and roast for 15–20 mins, until everything is piping hot.
Meanwhile, pick the parsley and dill leaves, discarding the stems. Thinly slice or mandolin the onion, then rinse under cold water.
Just before serving, toss the herbs and onion with the sumac, a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of salt.
Dish out the chicken and bean bake onto plates with the herb salad on the side. Dig in!

chefs.name: Mob
chefs.summary: Mob is on a mission to instil a love of cooking in everyone, because we believe in the positive impact that it can have on people's lives.
